title: potentilla nivea subsp. subquinata
intro: Potentilla nivea subsp. subquinata, also known as the alpine cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The herbal preparations of Potentilla nivea subsp. subquinata typically involve the use of its roots and leaves, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are said to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to protect against oxidative stress and promote wound healing.
